name: @aily-project/board-arduino_uno
description: The Arduino UNO R3 is an open-source prototyping platform based on the ATmega328P, featuring 14 digital I/O pins (6 with PWM output), 6 analog inputs, a 16 MHz crystal oscillator, USB-B connection, power jack, and ICSP header. It is one of the most popular Arduino boards for beginners.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_mega
description: The Arduino Mega is an open-source prototyping platform based on the ATmega2560, featuring 54 digital I/O pins (15 with PWM output), 16 analog inputs, 4 UARTs, a 16 MHz crystal oscillator, USB connection, power jack, and ICSP header.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_uno_r4_minima
description: The Arduino UNO R4 Minima features a powerful 32-bit Renesas RA4M1 microcontroller with increased processing power, expanded memory, and new onboard peripherals, while maintaining full compatibility with existing shields and accessories with no changes to the standard form factor or 5V operating voltage.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_uno_r4_wifi
description: The Arduino UNO R4 WiFi combines the processing power and new peripherals of the Renesas RA4M1 microcontroller with the wireless connectivity of the Espressif ESP32-S3. It also features a 12x8 LED matrix, Qwiic connector, VRTC, and OFF pin, meeting all the needs makers may encounter in their next project.

name: @aily-project/board-ai-vox
description: AI-VOX is an ESP32-S3-based AI voice assistant development board designed for the XiaoZhi AI chatbot project, supporting voice wake-up, streaming ASR + LLM + TTS voice interaction, Wi-Fi connectivity, OLED/LCD display, and MCP protocol-based smart device control.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_due
description: The Arduino Due is the first Arduino board based on a 32-bit ARM core microcontroller (Atmel SAM3X8E), featuring 54 digital I/O pins, 12 analog inputs, 4 UARTs, an 84 MHz clock, USB OTG, 2 DAC, 2 TWI, SPI, and JTAG. This configuration uses the Native USB port for programming.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_due_dbg
description: The Arduino Due is the first Arduino board based on a 32-bit ARM core microcontroller (Atmel SAM3X8E), featuring 54 digital I/O pins, 12 analog inputs, 4 UARTs, an 84 MHz clock, USB OTG, 2 DAC, 2 TWI, SPI, and JTAG. This configuration uses the Programming port for uploading.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_leonardo
description: The Arduino Leonardo is a microcontroller board based on the ATmega32U4 with built-in USB communication, featuring 20 digital I/O pins (7 as PWM outputs), 12 analog inputs, a 16 MHz crystal oscillator, micro USB connection, ICSP header, and reset button.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_micro
description: The Arduino Micro is a compact microcontroller board based on the ATmega32U4 with built-in USB, featuring 20 digital I/O pins, 12 analog inputs, and a 16 MHz crystal oscillator in a breadboard-friendly form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_nano
description: The Arduino Nano is a compact, breadboard-friendly board based on the ATmega328P, featuring 14 digital I/O pins (6 with PWM output), 8 analog inputs, a 16 MHz crystal oscillator, mini-B USB connection, and ICSP header.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_nano_esp32
description: The Arduino Nano ESP32 is based on the ESP32-S3 (Xtensa LX7 dual-core at 240 MHz) in the compact Nano form factor, featuring Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n and Bluetooth 5.0 LE connectivity, 8 MB Flash, 512 KB SRAM, up to 16 MB PSRAM, 14 digital I/O pins, 8 analog inputs, and USB-C. It is fully supported by the Arduino ecosystem and MicroPython.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_nano_r4
description: The Arduino Nano R4 brings the power of a 32-bit Arm Cortex-M4 processor to the beloved Nano form factor. Based on the Renesas RA4M1 microcontroller, it features 256 KB Flash, 32 KB SRAM, and 8 KB EEPROM running at 48 MHz, with a built-in real-time clock (RTC), 14-bit ADC, and 12-bit DAC.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_zero
description: The Arduino Zero is a simple and powerful 32-bit extension of the platform established by the UNO, based on the Atmel ATSAMD21G18A microcontroller, featuring 14 digital I/O pins (12 with PWM output), 6 analog inputs, a 48 MHz clock, native USB, 1 DAC, 1 UART, 1 SPI, 1 I2C, and an Atmel Embedded Debugger (EDBG). This configuration uses the Native USB port for programming.

name: @aily-project/board-beginner_a1
description: Beginner A1 is an entry-level learning board with two DC motors, two servos, and built-in ESP-NOW wireless communication.

name: @aily-project/board-df_esp32s3_aicam
description: The DFRobot ESP32-S3 AI CAM is a compact camera development board based on the ESP32-S3 chip, integrating an OV3660 camera module, LED, microphone, speaker, and infrared, designed for AI vision applications such as image recognition, object detection, and video streaming over Wi-Fi.

name: @aily-project/board-df_firebeetle2_esp32s3
description: The DFRobot FireBeetle 2 ESP32-S3 is a low-power IoT development board based on the ESP32-S3 dual-core processor, featuring Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity, onboard charging circuit, and GDI display interface, suitable for IoT and AI edge computing applications.

name: @aily-project/board-df_unihiker_k10
description: The UNIHIKER K10 is an ESP32-S3-based educational development board by DFRobot, featuring a 2.8-inch touchscreen display, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity, built-in camera, microphone, speaker, RGB indicator, and various sensors, designed for AI and IoT education.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32
description: Generic ESP32 development board configuration. The ESP32 is a dual-core Xtensa LX6 MCU with integrated 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi and Bluetooth dual-mode communication, supporting 802.11 b/g/n and Bluetooth v4.2 BR/EDR/BLE standards.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32c3
description: Generic ESP32-C3 development board configuration. The ESP32-C3 features a RISC-V 32-bit single-core processor, supporting 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5 (LE), providing industry-leading RF performance, robust security mechanisms, and ample memory resources for IoT applications.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32c5
description: Generic ESP32-C5 development board configuration. The ESP32-C5 features full Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth Low Energy, Zigbee, and Thread capabilities for versatile wireless connectivity.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32c6
description: Generic ESP32-C6 development board configuration. The ESP32-C6 supports 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5, and IEEE 802.15.4 (Zigbee 3.0 and Thread 1.3) wireless protocols.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32h2
description: Generic ESP32-H2 development board configuration. The ESP32-H2 integrates a 2.4 GHz receiver compatible with Bluetooth Low Energy and IEEE 802.15.4, supporting Bluetooth 5 (LE), Bluetooth Mesh, Zigbee, Thread, and Matter protocols for low-power IoT applications.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32p4
description: Generic ESP32-P4 development board configuration. The ESP32-P4 features a dual-core RISC-V processor at up to 400 MHz with AI instruction extensions, supporting USB 2.0, MIPI-CSI, MIPI-DSI, and other peripheral interfaces, suitable for HMI and AIoT product prototyping.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32s2
description: Generic ESP32-S2 development board configuration. The ESP32-S2 is a highly integrated, cost-effective, low-power, security-focused single-core Wi-Fi SoC with powerful capabilities and rich I/O interfaces.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32s3
description: Generic ESP32-S3 development board configuration. The ESP32-S3 is a dual-core Xtensa LX7 MCU integrating 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5 (LE) with Long Range mode support and AI acceleration capabilities.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32s3dev
description: Wifiduino ESP32S3dev board configuration with default pin mappings and settings.

name: @aily-project/board-luatos-esp32c3-core
description: The LuatOS ESP32-C3 Core is a compact development board by OpenLuat based on the ESP32-C3 RISC-V chip, featuring Wi-Fi and BLE 5.0 connectivity, dual onboard LEDs, and a breadboard-friendly form factor for IoT application development.

name: @aily-project/board-luatos-esp32s3-core
description: The LuatOS ESP32-S3 Core is a development board by OpenLuat based on the ESP32-S3 dual-core Xtensa LX7 chip, featuring Wi-Fi and BLE 5.0 connectivity, dual SPI, dual I2C interfaces, and enhanced AI computing capabilities.

name: @aily-project/board-mbrick-r
description: Generic ESP32-C3 development board configuration. The ESP32-C3 features a RISC-V 32-bit single-core processor, supporting 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5 (LE), providing industry-leading RF performance, robust security mechanisms, and ample memory resources for IoT applications.

name: @aily-project/board-metro_esp32s2
description: The Adafruit Metro ESP32-S2 is a Metro-form-factor development board based on the ESP32-S2 WROVER module with 4 MB Flash, 2 MB PSRAM, native USB, Wi-Fi, LiPoly battery charging, microSD slot, and STEMMA QT connector.

name: @aily-project/board-metro_esp32s3
description: The Adafruit Metro ESP32-S3 is a Metro-form-factor development board based on the ESP32-S3 with dual-core 240 MHz processor, 16 MB Flash, 8 MB PSRAM, native USB, Wi-Fi, BLE 5.0, LiPoly battery charging, microSD slot, and STEMMA QT connector.

name: @aily-project/board-metro_m4_airlift
description: The Adafruit Metro M4 AirLift is based on the ATSAMD51J19 (ARM Cortex-M4 @ 120 MHz) with an onboard ESP32 co-processor for WiFi/BLE, 2 MB QSPI Flash, NeoPixel, microSD slot, and STEMMA QT connector in a classic Metro form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-metro_m4_feat
description: The Adafruit Metro M4 Express featuring ATSAMD51J19 (ARM Cortex-M4 @ 120 MHz) with 2 MB QSPI Flash, NeoPixel, 2 DAC outputs, microSD slot, and full Arduino Uno R3 header layout.

name: @aily-project/board-metro_rp2040
description: The Adafruit Metro RP2040 is based on the RP2040 chip with dual-core Arm Cortex-M0+ processor at up to 133 MHz, 264 KB SRAM, 16 MB QSPI Flash, microSD slot, NeoPixel, STEMMA QT connector, and Metro Uno form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-metro_rp2350
description: The Adafruit Metro RP2350 is based on the RP2350 chip with dual-core Arm Cortex-M33 processor at up to 150 MHz, 520 KB SRAM, 16 MB QSPI Flash, 6 ADC inputs, microSD slot, NeoPixel, STEMMA QT connector, HSTX port, and Metro Uno form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-microbit
description: The BBC micro:bit v1 is a pocket-sized educational computer featuring a 32-bit ARM Cortex-M0 processor (Nordic nRF51822), 25 LED matrix display, 2 programmable buttons, accelerometer, compass, Bluetooth Low Energy, and edge connector for expansion.

name: @aily-project/board-microbit-v2
description: The BBC micro:bit v2 is an enhanced pocket-sized educational computer featuring a 64 MHz ARM Cortex-M4 processor (Nordic nRF52833) with FPU, 25 LED matrix display, 2 programmable buttons, built-in speaker and microphone, touch-sensitive logo, accelerometer, compass, and Bluetooth 5.0.

name: @aily-project/board-mpython2
description: The mPython Board 2.0 is an ESP32-based educational development board featuring a built-in OLED display, buzzer, light sensor, accelerometer, and various other sensors, designed for STEAM education and IoT projects.

name: @aily-project/board-mpython3
description: The mPython Board 3.0 is an ESP32-S3-based educational development board with enhanced processing power, RGB LEDs, programmable buttons, touch pins, dual SPI/I2C interfaces, designed for STEAM education and AI/IoT projects.

name: @aily-project/board-nl_esp32s3_cam
description: The NL-ESP32S3-CAM is an ESP32-S3-based camera development board with integrated camera module, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity, suitable for image capture, video streaming, and AI vision applications.

name: @aily-project/board-nrf52832_dk
description: The nRF52832 DK is a versatile single-board development kit for the nRF52832 SoC, supporting Bluetooth Low Energy, Bluetooth mesh, NFC, ANT and 2.4 GHz proprietary applications. It features a 64 MHz Cortex-M4F processor, 512 KB Flash, 64 KB RAM, and Arduino Uno Rev3 compatible headers.

name: @aily-project/board-nrf52840_dk
description: The nRF52840 DK is a versatile single-board development kit for the nRF52840 SoC, suitable for Bluetooth Low Energy, Bluetooth Mesh, Thread, Zigbee, 802.15.4, ANT, and 2.4 GHz proprietary applications. It is the recommended Nordic development kit for Amazon Sidewalk.

name: @aily-project/board-nrf52840_dongle
description: The nRF52840 Dongle is a small, low-cost USB dongle that supports Bluetooth 5.4, Bluetooth mesh, Thread, Zigbee, 802.15.4, ANT and 2.4 GHz proprietary protocols. It is the perfect target hardware for use with nRF Connect for Desktop.

name: @aily-project/board-ojbadge
description: OJBadge is an ESP32-S3 smart terminal with color screen, touch control, I2S audio, battery management, supporting USB and Wi-Fi/Bluetooth.

name: @aily-project/board-oj_cool_class_a1
description: Cool Class A1 - ESP32-based development board with built-in WS2812 RGB LED, programmable buttons, and voice recognition module for creative and interactive projects.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32_aiot_kit
description: The ESP32-S3 AIoT Kit is a comprehensive all-in-one development kit based on the ESP32-S3 module designed for AI and IoT learning. It integrates 27 sub-modules including analog joystick, DHT11 sensor, BMI270 IMU, light sensors, buzzer, LEDs, INA219 power monitor, TB6612 motor driver, 1.3-inch IPS LCD, 0.96-inch OLED, thermistor, rotary encoder, VL53L0X laser ranging, SD card, PIR sensor, buttons, PCM digital microphone, MAX98357 amplifier, and OV2640 camera module.

name: @aily-project/board-wifiduino_c3mini
description: Wifiduino C3mini is a compact development board based on the ESP32-C3 RISC-V chip with Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5.0 LE connectivity, suitable for IoT applications and rapid prototyping.

name: @aily-project/board-wifiduino_esp32dev
description: Wifiduino ESP32 development board based on the ESP32 dual-core Xtensa LX6 processor with integrated Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity for IoT and embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-wifiduino_cbot19
description: Wifiduino Cbot19 is a development board based on the ESP32-S3 chip, designed for artificial intelligence and IoT application development with Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity.

name: @aily-project/board-esp32s3basic
description: The ESP32-S3 AIoT Basic Kit is an all-in-one development kit based on the ESP32-S3 module designed for AI and IoT learning. It integrates 9 sub-modules including buttons, buzzer, LED, light sensor, LCD, digital microphone, SD card, audio amplifier, and camera module, with comprehensive tutorials and learning resources.

name: @aily-project/board-jinniu_board
description: The JinNiu (Golden Bull) Creative Wing Board is an ATmega328P-based education development board designed for K-12 STEAM learning. It integrates LED, WS2812 RGB LEDs, button, potentiometer, DHT11 temperature/humidity sensor, light sensor, motor driver, ultrasonic sensor, and supports servo/motor interfaces for robotics and programming education projects.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_mega_pro
description: The Zduino MEGA2560 Pro is an enhanced Arduino Mega-compatible board based on the ATmega2560, featuring 54 digital I/O pins (14 with PWM output), 16 analog inputs, a 16 MHz crystal oscillator, USB connection, power jack, and ICSP header.

name: @aily-project/board-oj_stm32f103c8_nano
description: OpenJumper STM32F103C8 Nano development board with Arm Cortex-M3 core at 72 MHz, 64 KB Flash, 20 KB SRAM, rich peripheral interfaces for embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-summer-v2
description: The Summer v2 is a development board supporting encoder motors, TT motors, servos, ultrasonic sensors, Sentry2 obstacle avoidance, IR remote control, buzzer, OLED display, and stepper motor control, suitable for smart hardware and robotics applications.

name: @aily-project/board-arduino_uno_r3_pro
description: The Zduino unoR3 Pro is an enhanced Arduino UNO-compatible board based on the ATmega328P, featuring 14 digital I/O pins (6 with PWM output), 6 analog inputs, a 16 MHz crystal oscillator, USB connection, power jack, and ICSP header.

name: @aily-project/board-zduino_unor4_wifi_pro
description: The Zduino UNO R4 WIFI Pro is an enhanced Arduino UNO R4-compatible board featuring a powerful 32-bit Renesas RA4M1 microcontroller with increased processing power, expanded memory, and onboard wireless connectivity, while maintaining full compatibility with existing shields and the standard 5V operating voltage.

name: @aily-project/board-wifiduino_v2
description: The Wifiduino v2 is based on the ESP32-C3 chip with a 160 MHz RISC-V processor, 400 KB SRAM, 4 MB Flash, Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n, Bluetooth 5.0 LE, 18 digital/analog I/O pins, 12-bit ADC, and UART/SPI/I2C/USB interfaces, suitable for mid-range low-power IoT applications.

name: @aily-project/board-wifiduino32
description: The Wifiduino 32 is based on the ESP32-WROOM-32E module with a 240 MHz dual-core processor, 520 KB SRAM, 4 MB Flash, Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n, Bluetooth 4.2 BR/EDR + BLE, 22 digital/analog I/O pins, 12-bit ADC, 8-bit DAC, and UART/SPI/I2C/USB interfaces, suitable for complex IoT devices and multi-task processing.

name: @aily-project/board-wifiduino32s3
description: The Wifiduino 32S3 is based on the ESP32-S3-N16R8 module with a 240 MHz dual-core processor, 512 KB SRAM, 16 MB Flash, Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n with Wi-Fi 6 features, Bluetooth 5.2 LE + Mesh, 24 digital/analog I/O pins, 12-bit ADC and DAC, and UART/SPI/I2C/USB interfaces, ideal for AIoT and industrial automation projects.

name: @aily-project/board-raspberrypi_pico
description: The Raspberry Pi Pico is a low-cost microcontroller board based on the RP2040 chip with a dual-core Arm Cortex-M0+ processor at up to 133 MHz, 264 KB SRAM, 2 MB onboard Flash, 26 multi-function GPIO pins (3 analog inputs), SPI, I2C, UART, and a unique Programmable I/O (PIO) subsystem for custom peripheral support.

name: @aily-project/board-raspberrypi_pico_w
description: The Raspberry Pi Pico W is based on the RP2040 chip with a dual-core Arm Cortex-M0+ processor at up to 133 MHz, 264 KB SRAM, 2 MB Flash, built-in 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi (802.11n) and Bluetooth 5.2 (BLE) via the Infineon CYW43439, 26 multi-function GPIO pins, SPI, I2C, UART, ADC, and PWM, ideal for IoT and wireless projects.

name: @aily-project/board-raspberrypi_pico2
description: The Raspberry Pi Pico 2 is based on the RP2350 chip with dual-core Arm Cortex-M33 or RISC-V Hazard3 processors at up to 150 MHz, 520 KB SRAM, 4 MB onboard Flash, 26 multi-function GPIO pins, SPI, I2C, UART, ADC, PWM, and enhanced security features over its predecessor.

name: @aily-project/board-raspberrypi_pico2_w
description: The Raspberry Pi Pico 2 W is a wireless-enabled board based on the RP2350 chip with dual Arm Cortex-M33 / RISC-V Hazard3 cores at up to 150 MHz, 520 KB SRAM, 4 MB Flash, 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi 4 (802.11n) and Bluetooth 5.2 (BLE) via the Infineon CYW43439, 26 GPIO pins, and rich peripherals (SPI, I2C, UART, ADC, PWM, PIO) for IoT and wireless embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-grove_beginner_kit
description: The Grove Beginner Kit for Arduino is an all-in-one starter kit powered by an Arduino-compatible Seeeduino Lotus board with 10 additional Grove sensors integrated on a single board. No breadboard, soldering, or wiring needed — just focus on coding and learning Arduino. Modules can also be detached and connected via Grove cables for further exploration.

name: @aily-project/board-seeed_lotus
description: The Seeeduino Lotus is an ATmega328P-based development board that combines Seeeduino and Grove Base Shield on a single board. It features 14 digital I/O pins (6 with PWM), 7 analog inputs, 12 onboard Grove connectors for plug-and-play sensor integration, micro USB, ICSP header, running at 16 MHz. V1.1 uses CP2102N for Mac compatibility.

name: @aily-project/board-sd_wio_terminal
description: The Seeed Studio Wio Terminal is a SAMD51-based microcontroller with wireless connectivity powered by Realtek RTL8720DN. It features a 2.4" LCD (320x240), built-in IMU, microphone, buzzer, microSD card slot, light sensor, IR emitter, 5-way switch, 3 user buttons, and a 40-pin GPIO header.

name: @aily-project/board-seekfree-bot
description: The Seekfree Robot B1 is an ESP32-S3-based smart robot car kit designed for robotics education and competition, with motor control, sensor integration, Wi-Fi/BLE connectivity, and programmable capabilities.

name: @aily-project/board-c011f6
description: STM32C011F6 development board with Arm Cortex-M0+ core at 48 MHz, 32 KB Flash, 6 KB SRAM, 20-pin TSSOP package, suitable for cost-effective embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-c031c6
description: STM32C031C6 development board with Arm Cortex-M0+ core at 48 MHz, 32 KB Flash, 12 KB SRAM, 48-pin LQFP package, suitable for cost-effective embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-c051c8
description: STM32C051C8 development board with Arm Cortex-M0+ core at 48 MHz, 64 KB Flash, 12 KB SRAM, 48-pin LQFP package, suitable for cost-effective embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f103c8
description: STM32F103C8 development board with Arm Cortex-M3 core at 72 MHz, 64 KB Flash, 20 KB SRAM, 48-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f103rc
description: STM32F103RC development board with Arm Cortex-M3 core at 72 MHz, 256 KB Flash, 48 KB SRAM, 64-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f103re
description: STM32F103RE development board with Arm Cortex-M3 core at 72 MHz, 512 KB Flash, 64 KB SRAM, 64-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f103vc
description: STM32F103VC development board with Arm Cortex-M3 core at 72 MHz, 256 KB Flash, 48 KB SRAM, 100-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f103ze
description: STM32F103ZE development board with Arm Cortex-M3 core at 72 MHz, 512 KB Flash, 64 KB SRAM, 144-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f407ve
description: STM32F407VE development board with Arm Cortex-M4 core (with FPU) at 168 MHz, 512 KB Flash, 192 KB SRAM, 100-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for high-performance embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f407vg
description: STM32F407VG development board with Arm Cortex-M4 core (with FPU) at 168 MHz, 1 MB Flash, 192 KB SRAM, 100-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for high-performance embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f407ze
description: STM32F407ZE development board with Arm Cortex-M4 core (with FPU) at 168 MHz, 512 KB Flash, 192 KB SRAM, 144-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for high-performance embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32f407zg
description: STM32F407ZG development board with Arm Cortex-M4 core (with FPU) at 168 MHz, 1 MB Flash, 192 KB SRAM, 144-pin LQFP package, rich peripheral interfaces for high-performance embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32g0
description: STM32G0 Series development board featuring an Arm Cortex-M0+ core at up to 64 MHz, 16-512 KB Flash, 8-144 KB RAM, with rich peripheral interfaces suitable for cost-effective embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32g4
description: STM32G4 Series development board featuring an Arm Cortex-M4 core with FPU at up to 170 MHz, 32-512 KB Flash, 32-128 KB RAM, with advanced analog features suitable for mixed-signal and motor control applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32h5
description: STM32H5 Series development board featuring an Arm Cortex-M33 core at up to 250 MHz, 128 KB - 2 MB Flash, 32-640 KB RAM, with enhanced security features suitable for high-performance embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32h7
description: STM32H7 Series development board featuring an Arm Cortex-M7 core at up to 480 MHz, 128 KB - 2 MB Flash, 32 KB - 1 MB RAM, with rich peripheral interfaces suitable for compute-intensive embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32l0
description: STM32L0 Series development board featuring an ultra-low-power Arm Cortex-M0+ core at up to 32 MHz, 16-192 KB Flash, 2-20 KB RAM, with rich peripheral interfaces suitable for battery-powered and low-power embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32l1
description: STM32L1 Series development board featuring an ultra-low-power Arm Cortex-M3 core at up to 32 MHz, 32-512 KB Flash, 4-80 KB RAM, with rich peripheral interfaces suitable for energy-efficient embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32l4
description: STM32L4 Series development board featuring an ultra-low-power Arm Cortex-M4 core with FPU at up to 80 MHz, 128-1024 KB Flash, 40-320 KB RAM, optimized for low-power performance in embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32l5
description: STM32L5 Series development board featuring an Arm Cortex-M33 core with TrustZone at up to 110 MHz, 256-512 KB Flash, 256 KB RAM, with enhanced security features suitable for secure low-power embedded applications.

name: @aily-project/board-stm32wb
description: STM32WB Series development board featuring a dual-core architecture with Arm Cortex-M4 (up to 64 MHz) and Cortex-M0+ cores, 320 KB - 1 MB Flash, 48-256 KB RAM. Supports Bluetooth Low Energy, Zigbee, Thread, and Matter wireless connectivity protocols.

name: @aily-project/board-teensy40
description: The Teensy 4.0 is a development board featuring the NXP iMXRT1062 ARM Cortex-M7 processor running at 600 MHz, with 1024 KB RAM, 2048 KB Flash, 40 digital I/O pins (31 PWM-capable), 14 analog inputs, 7 serial ports, 3 SPI, 3 I2C, 2 I2S, and native USB. Designed by PJRC, the Teensy 4.0 offers exceptional performance in a compact 1.4 x 0.7 inch USB-C form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-teensy41
description: The Teensy 4.1 is a high-performance development board featuring the NXP iMXRT1062 ARM Cortex-M7 processor at 600 MHz, with 1024 KB RAM, 8 MB Flash, 55 digital I/O pins (35 PWM-capable), 18 analog inputs, 8 hardware serial ports, 3 SPI, 3 I2C, built-in 10/100 Mbit Ethernet PHY, micro SD card socket, USB host port, and pads for optional PSRAM/Flash. Designed by PJRC in a 2.4 x 0.7 inch form factor with USB-C connector.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_esp32c3
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32C3 is an ultra-compact board based on the ESP32-C3 RISC-V single-core processor at 160 MHz, with 4 MB Flash, 400 KB SRAM, Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n and Bluetooth 5.0 LE, 11 GPIO pins, battery charging support, and deep sleep as low as 44 uA, in the tiny XIAO form factor (21x17.8 mm).

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_esp32c5
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32C5 is based on the ESP32-C5 RISC-V single-core processor at up to 240 MHz, supporting Wi-Fi 6 (2.4 GHz), Bluetooth 5.0 LE, Zigbee 3.0, and Thread 1.3 protocols, with 4 MB Flash, low-power deep sleep mode (as low as 14 uA), lithium battery charge management, and a compact XIAO form factor (21x17.8 mm).

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_esp32c6
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32C6 is based on the ESP32-C6 RISC-V single-core processor at up to 160 MHz, supporting Wi-Fi 6 (2.4 GHz), Bluetooth 5.0 LE, Zigbee 3.0, and Thread 1.3 (IEEE 802.15.4) protocols, with 4 MB Flash, low-power deep sleep mode (as low as 15 uA), lithium battery charge management, and a compact XIAO form factor (21x17.8 mm).

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_esp32s3
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32S3 features the ESP32-S3 Xtensa LX7 dual-core processor at 240 MHz, with 8 MB Flash, Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n and Bluetooth 5.0 LE, 11 GPIO pins, USB-C, deep sleep as low as 14 uA, lithium battery charge management, and AI vector acceleration capabilities in the ultra-compact XIAO form factor (21x17.8 mm).

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_esp32s3_plus
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32S3 Plus features the ESP32-S3 Xtensa LX7 dual-core processor at 240 MHz, with 16 MB Flash, 8 MB PSRAM, Wi-Fi 802.11 b/g/n and Bluetooth 5.0 LE, extended GPIO pins, USB-C, deep sleep as low as 14 uA, and lithium battery charge management. An enhanced version of the XIAO ESP32S3 with more memory and additional I/O in a compact form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_esp32s3_sense
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32S3 Sense features the ESP32-S3 Xtensa LX7 dual-core processor at 240 MHz, with 8 MB Flash, 8 MB PSRAM, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth 5.0 LE, integrated OV2640 camera sensor, digital microphone, and external SD card slot. Designed for embedded machine learning applications such as intelligent voice and vision AI.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_mg24
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO MG24 features the Silicon Labs EFR32MG24 ARM Cortex-M33 at 78 MHz with built-in AI/ML hardware accelerator MVP, supporting Matter, Thread, Zigbee, Bluetooth LE 5.3, and Bluetooth Mesh in the ultra-compact 21x17.8 mm XIAO form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_nrf52840
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO nRF52840 features the powerful Nordic nRF52840 MCU with Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity in an ultra-compact 21x17.8 mm form factor, suitable for wearable devices and IoT projects.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_nrf54l15
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO nRF54L15 features the Nordic nRF54L15 MCU with Arm Cortex-M33 at 128 MHz and RISC-V coprocessor, supporting Bluetooth LE 6.0, Thread, Zigbee, Matter, and NFC in an ultra-compact 21x17.8 mm form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_ra4m1
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO RA4M1 features the Renesas RA4M1 microcontroller (32-bit ARM Cortex-M4, up to 48 MHz) with 256 KB Flash, 32 KB SRAM, 8 KB EEPROM, 19 GPIO pins, built-in RGB LED, ultra-low-power design (45 uA deep sleep), hardware encryption, and battery management.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_rp2040
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO RP2040 is a compact microcontroller board based on the Raspberry Pi RP2040 chip, featuring a dual-core ARM Cortex-M0+ processor at up to 133 MHz, 264 KB SRAM, 2 MB Flash, 11 multi-function GPIO pins, and USB-C, in Seeed Studio's tiny XIAO form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_rp2350
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO RP2350 is a compact microcontroller board based on the Raspberry Pi RP2350 chip, featuring dual-core ARM Cortex-M33 / RISC-V Hazard3 processors at up to 150 MHz, 520 KB SRAM, 2 MB Flash, USB-C, and enhanced security features in Seeed Studio's tiny XIAO form factor.

name: @aily-project/board-xiao_samd21
description: The Seeed Studio XIAO SAMD21 (Seeeduino XIAO) is an ultra-compact microcontroller board based on the Atmel ATSAMD21G18A ARM Cortex-M0+ at 48 MHz, featuring 11 digital/analog GPIO pins, 256 KB Flash, 32 KB SRAM, native USB, 1 DAC, 1 UART, 1 SPI, 1 I2C, in the tiny 21x17.8 mm XIAO form factor.
